17	// Definition describes an executable metric runtime record.
18	type Definition struct {
19		DefinitionID string            `json:"definition_id"`
20		MetricID     string            `json:"metric_id"`
21		Command      []string          `json:"command"`

30	// Thresholds describe the target values used for pass/fail evaluation.
31	type Thresholds struct {

38	type HistoryRecord struct {

37	// ParseSince converts a CLI/query window string into a cutoff time.
38	// Empty input returns the zero time and no error.
39	func ParseSince(raw string) (time.Time, error) {
40		raw = strings.TrimSpace(raw)
41		if raw == "" {
42			return time.Time{}, nil
43		}
44	
45		now := time.Now()
46		switch raw {
47		case "today":
48			return time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location()), nil
49		case "now":
50			return now, nil
51		}
52	
53		if strings.HasSuffix(raw, "d") {
54			n, err := parsePositiveInt(strings.TrimSuffix(raw, "d"))
55			if err != nil {
56				return time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("expected Nd window, got %q", raw)
57			}
58			return now.AddDate(0, 0, -n), nil
59		}
60	
61		for _, layout := range []string{time.RFC3339, "2006-01-02"} {
62			if parsed, err := time.Parse(layout, raw); err == nil {
63				return parsed, nil
64			}
65		}

81	// computeTierSuccess scans .ddx/executions/*/result.json under workingDir and
82	// returns per-tier aggregates. When lastN > 0, only the most recent lastN
83	// attempts (sorted by attempt directory name, which is a sortable timestamp)
84	// are considered.
85	func computeTierSuccess(workingDir string, lastN int) ([]tierSuccessRow, error) {
86		execRoot := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "executions")
87		entries, err := os.ReadDir(execRoot)
88		if err != nil {
89			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
90				return []tierSuccessRow{}, nil
91			}
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read executions dir: %w", err)
93		}
94	
95		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
96		for _, entry := range entries {
97			if entry.IsDir() {
98				names = append(names, entry.Name())
99			}
100		}
101		// Directory names are sortable timestamps (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hash>), so
102		// lexicographic sort is chronological.
103		sort.Strings(names)
104	
105		// First pass: read and keep only usable results, preserving chronological
106		// order. --last N then picks the most recent N usable attempts so that
107		// malformed or missing files never hide a valid recent attempt.
108		type loadedResult struct {
109			harness     string
110			model       string
111			outcome     string
112			failureMode string
113			costUSD     float64
114			durMS       int
115		}
116		loaded := make([]loadedResult, 0, len(names))
117		for _, name := range names {
118			resultPath := filepath.Join(execRoot, name, "result.json")
119			raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
120			if err != nil {
121				continue
122			}
123			var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
124			if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
125				continue
126			}
127			if res.Harness == "" {
128				continue
129			}
130			loaded = append(loaded, loadedResult{
131				harness:     res.Harness,
132				model:       res.Model,
133				outcome:     res.Outcome,
134				failureMode: res.FailureMode,
135				costUSD:     res.CostUSD,
136				durMS:       res.DurationMS,
137			})
138		}
139		if lastN > 0 && len(loaded) > lastN {
140			loaded = loaded[len(loaded)-lastN:]
141		}
142	
143		type agg struct {
144			harness          string
145			model            string
146			attempts         int
147			successes        int
148			totalCostUSD     float64
149			wastedCostUSD    float64
150			effectiveCostUSD float64
151			totalDurMS       float64
152			failureModes     map[string]int
153		}
154		byTier := map[string]*agg{}
155		order := []string{}
156	
157		for _, res := range loaded {
158			tier := tierKey(res.harness, res.model)
159			a, ok := byTier[tier]
160			if !ok {
161				a = &agg{harness: res.harness, model: res.model, failureModes: map[string]int{}}
162				byTier[tier] = a
163				order = append(order, tier)
164			}
165			a.attempts++
166			if res.outcome == "task_succeeded" {
167				a.successes++
168				a.effectiveCostUSD += res.costUSD
169			} else {
170				a.wastedCostUSD += res.costUSD
171			}
172			if res.failureMode != "" {
173				a.failureModes[res.failureMode]++
174			}
175			a.totalCostUSD += res.costUSD
176			a.totalDurMS += float64(res.durMS)
177		}
178	
179		rows := make([]tierSuccessRow, 0, len(order))
180		for _, tier := range order {
181			a := byTier[tier]
182			row := tierSuccessRow{
183				Tier:             tier,
184				Harness:          a.harness,
185				Model:            a.model,
186				Attempts:         a.attempts,
187				Successes:        a.successes,
188				WastedCostUSD:    a.wastedCostUSD,
189				EffectiveCostUSD: a.effectiveCostUSD,
190			}
191			if a.attempts > 0 {
192				row.SuccessRate = float64(a.successes) / float64(a.attempts)
193				row.AvgCostUSD = a.totalCostUSD / float64(a.attempts)
194				row.AvgDurationMS = a.totalDurMS / float64(a.attempts)
195			}
196			if len(a.failureModes) > 0 {
197				row.FailureModes = a.failureModes
198			}
199			rows = append(rows, row)
200		}

218	func (f *CommandFactory) checkForUpdates(cmd *cobra.Command) {
219		// Check if disabled via env var
220		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
221			return
222		}
223	
224		// Load config
225		cfg, err := config.Load()
226		if err != nil {
227			// Silent failure - use defaults
228			cfg = config.DefaultNewConfig()
229		}
230	
231		// Check if disabled via config
232		if cfg.UpdateCheck != nil && !cfg.UpdateCheck.Enabled {
233			return
234		}

236		// Create checker and perform check (synchronous)
237		checker := update.NewChecker(f.Version, cfg)

239		f.updateChecker = checker
240		f.updateDone = make(chan struct{})
241		done := f.updateDone
242		f.updateMu.Unlock()

244		// Run the check asynchronously so command startup is never blocked.
245		go func() {
246			defer close(done)
247			_, _ = checker.CheckForUpdate(context.Background())
248			// Also refresh plugin version cache while we have a network call in flight.
249			f.refreshPluginVersionCache()

331	func (f *CommandFactory) checkVersionGate(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
332		// Dev builds bypass the gate

337		// Read project versions

353		}
354		if needsUpgrade {
355			return fmt.Errorf("this project requires DDx v%s or newer (you have %s).\nRun 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary",
356				strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
357				f.Version)

415				continue
416			}
417			needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(entry.Version, latestVersion)
418			if err == nil && needsUpgrade {

426	// refreshPluginVersionCache fetches the latest version for each installed plugin
427	// from GitHub and writes the result to the plugin cache. Called from the
428	// background update goroutine — failures are silent.
429	func (f *CommandFactory) refreshPluginVersionCache() {
430		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
431		_ = cache.Load() // ignore missing-file error
432	
433		if !cache.IsExpired() {
434			return
435		}
436	
437		state, err := registry.LoadState()
438		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
439			return
440		}
441	
442		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
443		updated := false
444		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
445			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
446			if err != nil {
447				continue
448			}
449			release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source)
450			if err != nil {
451				continue
452			}
453			latestVersion := strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
454			cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name] = latestVersion
455			updated = true
456		}
457	
458		if updated {
459			cache.Data.LastCheck = time.Now()
460			_ = cache.Save()
461		}
462	}

580		if err != nil {
581			return nil

592			return nil
593		}

597			entry.Prompt = bodies.Prompt
598			entry.Response = bodies.Response
599			entry.Stderr = bodies.Stderr
